_note: Miami HQ data — Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens. 4 group stage matches.

venue.name: Hard Rock Stadium
venue.address: 347 Don Shula Dr, Miami Gardens, FL 33056
venue.notes: Clear bag policy strictly enforced. No re-entry once inside. Gates open 2.5 hours before kickoff.
venue.parkingNote: On-site parking available from $50. Book in advance via SeatGeek or the official FIFA app. Lots fill 3+ hours early.

transit.recommended: Rideshare (Uber/Lyft) or personal vehicle — stadium is in Miami Gardens, ~16 miles from Downtown Miami
lines: No direct rail. Miami-Dade Transit Route 27 stops near the stadium.
tips: Rideshare is the most practical option. Designate a pickup zone before the match ends.
Metrorail + bus combo from Dadeland South: 45–60 min. Not ideal for large groups.
Parking lots on Stephens Island and the east side fill earliest — arrive 2+ hours early if driving.
Wynwood and Brickell are 30+ min away by car on match days — head out early.
transit.parking: On-site parking $50–$80. Reserve via FIFA official app or SeatGeek. Cash lots available but limited.
transit.rideshare: Designated Uber/Lyft pickup on the stadium's east side, Gate H area. Expect surge pricing post-match.

fanZones.name: FIFA Fan Festival — Bayfront Park
fanZones.notes: Free entry. Massive main stage, food vendors, live music, giant screens. Best pre-match energy in the city.

fanZones.name: Wynwood Fan Zone
fanZones.notes: Outdoor watch party in the arts district. Multiple screens, food trucks. Check local listings for confirmation.

preGame.name: Wynwood Walls area bars
preGame.notes: Best pre-game energy in Miami. Bars along NW 2nd Ave pack out hours before kickoff. International fan mix.

preGame.name: Brickell City Centre
preGame.notes: Upscale pre-game option. Pull & Pour, Sugar, and Silverbar attract a soccer crowd on match days.

preGame.name: Coconut Grove
preGame.notes: Relaxed waterfront vibe. Good for earlier arrivals who want to avoid peak-hour traffic heading north.

cityEnergyForecast.crowdNote: Saudi Arabia vs Uruguay — Group H opener. Miami's large South American community will bring early tournament energy. Uruguay fans passionate; Saudi diaspora adds color.
Uruguay vs Cape Verde — Group H Matchday 2. Smaller fan bases but competitive football. Good atmosphere for a weekend afternoon match.
Scotland vs Brazil — Group C finale. Brazil's Miami diaspora is one of the city's largest. Scottish fans travel in force. Hard Rock will be electric.
Colombia vs Portugal — Group K finale. Miami's Colombian and Portuguese communities will fill Hard Rock. Cristiano Ronaldo on the biggest stage in the city.

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Saudi Arabia vs Uruguay — Sunday afternoon match. I-95 manageable but rideshare pricing will spike post-match.
tips: Rideshare is easiest — designate pickup zone before leaving your seat
I-95 northbound clear by 9pm if you stay for post-match atmosphere
Parking available but book in advance via FIFA app

transitPain.label: Low
transitPain.note: Uruguay vs Cape Verde — most manageable match-day logistics of Miami's schedule. Mid-week afternoon.
tips: Best day to test your route if attending multiple matches
I-95 express lanes run smoothly on weekend afternoons
Post-match rideshare wait under 15 min historically

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: Scotland vs Brazil — Miami's biggest group-stage match. Brazilian diaspora + Scottish travelers pack every road and transit corridor.
tips: Leave Miami proper 2+ hours before kickoff — this is maximum crowd
Scottish fans in full kit arrive from 3pm; atmosphere builds early
Post-match rideshare surge — walk east of the stadium to secondary pickup zone
Monitor Waze: 826 or Turnpike may be faster than I-95

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: Colombia vs Portugal — tournament farewell weekend, Group K finale. City-wide energy across all corridors.
tips: Book post-match transport before the match starts
Colombian and Portuguese communities will be in full voice from 5pm
Night match — Wynwood and Brickell will be packed until 2am

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Saudi Arabia vs Uruguay at Hard Rock — the tournament opener for Miami. Uruguay's passionate traveling support and Saudi diaspora make for a lively atmosphere. Good value for a Group H opener.

shouldIGoScores.label: Consider
shouldIGoScores.summary: Uruguay vs Cape Verde — competitive Group H football, best ticket value of Miami's schedule. Smaller crowd means a more relaxed matchday experience.

shouldIGoScores.label: Don't miss it
shouldIGoScores.summary: Scotland vs Brazil in Miami is everything. Miami's Brazilian community turns Hard Rock gold and green, Scottish fans arrive in full force, and the stadium becomes the loudest in the tournament. Group C finale with massive stakes.

shouldIGoScores.label: Strongly Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Colombia vs Portugal — Cristiano Ronaldo at Hard Rock Stadium is a once-per-generation moment. Miami's Latin American and Iberian communities make this the city's most anticipated Group K fixture.
